seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: input+goal: generalize the forest/tree to a GENERAL connected pipe graph with cross-pipes (cycles) so radius-r covering becomes NP-hard (min-cost covering is polynomial on trees), skin as aquarium circulation-pump placement, add skewed per-location installation costs correlated with hub degree; objective stays minimize total cost to serve every tank within pipe-hop radius r. Baseline B = install-in-every-tank; score = B / achieved_cost.
title: Reef Aquarium Plumbing: Minimum-Cost Circulation Pump Placement
strategies: install-everywhere reference (reproduces checker baseline B, Ratio=0.1)
max-coverage greedy set cover: repeatedly install where the most currently-unserved tanks get served (cost-blind)
cost-effective greedy: repeatedly install where newly-served-tanks / cost is largest
redundancy pruning: drop the most expensive installed pumps that are no longer needed for full coverage
multi-start greedy + local-search swaps keeping the cheapest feasible cover
